study: Stress FT erosion vs Frozen LP comparison under per-recording DASS labels
question: Does fine-tuning LaBraM on UCSD Stress per-recording DASS labels beat a frozen linear probe on the same features, or does it actively erode the pretrained representation?

headline.verdict: EROSION. Frozen LP (0.605 ± 0.030 across 8 seeds) beats real-label FT (0.443 ± 0.068 across 3 seeds) by +16.1 pp. Real-label FT is statistically indistinguishable from training on shuffled labels (null mean 0.497 ± 0.081, p=0.70 one-sided). Fine-tuning under weak cross-subject DASS labels actively degrades the pretrained encoder's linearly-separable stress signal.

frozen_lp.description: Linear probe on cached mean-pooled LaBraM features (no fine-tuning).
frozen_lp.protocol: Subject-level StratifiedGroupKFold(5), LogisticRegression (C=1.0, class_weight=balanced) on StandardScaler-normed 200-d LaBraM features.

ft_real.description: Fine-tune LaBraM on real per-recording DASS labels. Canonical recipe: lr=1e-5, encoder_lr_scale=0.1, llrd=1.0 or 0.65, epochs=50, patience=15, warmup_freeze_epochs=1, loss=focal, aug_overlap=0.75.

ft_null.description: Label-permutation null. For each perm_seed in [0..9] we shuffle recording-level labels (preserving class balance) before CV and train under the same canonical recipe + training seed=42. Enabled via new --permute-labels flag in train_ft.py.

ft_drift_check.description: Reproducibility check. HEAD code + canonical recipe + subject-dass label + seed=42 should reproduce the archived canonical 0.6559 BA. Any gap indicates cuDNN non-determinism or hidden code drift.
ft_drift_check.root_cause: No functional code drift. pipeline/dataset.py, baseline/labram/, and src/model.py are unchanged since 6defff4. train_ft.py only added cross-dataset branching that does not touch the stress path. The 20.5 pp gap is attributable to cuDNN non-determinism: train_ft.py:1053-1056 seeds numpy+torch but does NOT set torch.backends.cudnn.deterministic=True, and the 70-recording class-imbalanced regime amplifies small init perturbations into 10-20 pp BA swings across runs. The archived 0.6559 is a lucky-tail draw, not a reproducible ceiling.

notes: All FT runs were archived subject-dass experiments before 2026-04-09.

This study uses the per-recording DASS label (Group column, matches Lin et al. 2025 protocol) instead of our previous --label subject-dass OR-aggregation which was reclassified as a trait-memorization artifact on 2026-04-09.
The 0.6161 ± 0.0292 number in snapshot_2026-04-09_analysis.json is the 3-seed subset; the more robust 8-seed estimate is 0.6049 ± 0.0299.
